role: Configuration directory containing per-language AST extraction rule definitions for the ArchSpine mirror system.
responsibility: Provides a complete set of YAML rule files that define how to detect imports, exports, and usages for each supported programming language (C/C++, Elixir, Go, Java, Kotlin, PHP, Python, Ruby, Rust, Scala, Swift, TypeScript). These rules are loaded by the ArchSpine scanner to perform language-specific static analysis and build symbol maps.

children.role: Defines AST extraction rules for the C/C++ programming language within the ArchSpine scanning pipeline.
Defines AST extraction patterns for the Elixir programming language within the ArchSpine mirror system.
Defines AST extraction patterns for the Go programming language within the ArchSpine mirror system.
Defines Java-specific AST extraction patterns for ArchSpine's code analysis pipeline.
Defines AST extraction rules for Kotlin language syntax within the ArchSpine mirror system
Language-specific pattern definition for PHP code analysis within the ArchSpine mirror system.
Defines AST extraction rules for the ArchSpine scanner to detect imports, exports, and usages in Python source code.

This document defines the architecture, behavior, and protocol of ArchSpine, a mirror system that creates and maintains a synchronized, governance-aware representation of a software repository for both human and AI consumption.

Defines the Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) extraction rules for the Rust language within the ArchSpine mirror system, enabling automatic recognition of code structures such as imports, exports (functions, structs, traits, implementations, variables), and usage patterns (function calls and path references).
Scala AST extraction rule configuration for ArchSpine

Defines the AST extraction patterns for the Swift programming language within the ArchSpine mirror system, enabling the automatic analysis and capture of Swift code structures for repository mirroring and documentation.
Defines the grammar for extracting TypeScript symbols and relationships in the ArchSpine AST pipeline.
